title: lichen_l2.c: No bounds check on frame_len returned by lichen_link_tx()
description:
## Dataflow trace

**Source:** `lichen_link_tx()` return value (lichen_l2.c:577-578)

**Sink:** `lichen_lora_l2_tx(tx_frame_buf, frame_len)` (lichen_l2.c:589)

## Issue

The code passes `frame_len = sizeof(tx_frame_buf)` (255 bytes) to `lichen_link_tx()` as an in/out parameter. The function returns the built frame size in `frame_len`.

There is no validation that the returned `frame_len` is bounded by the original buffer size:

```c
size_t frame_len = sizeof(tx_frame_buf);  // 255 bytes
ret = lichen_link_tx(&link_ctx, tx_ipv6_buf, pkt_len, NULL,
                     tx_frame_buf, &frame_len);
// No check that frame_len <= sizeof(tx_frame_buf) here
...
ret = lichen_lora_l2_tx(tx_frame_buf, frame_len);  // potential overread
```

## Mitigation

`lichen_lora_l2_tx()` has a check at lora_l2.c:561-564:
```c
if (len > LICHEN_LORA_MAX_PHY_PAYLOAD) {
    LOG_ERR("Packet too large: %zu (max %d)", len, LICHEN_LORA_MAX_PHY_PAYLOAD);
    return -EMSGSIZE;
}
```

This means an oversized `frame_len` would be caught and rejected. However, the error logging would reference data read from beyond the buffer in `LOG_ERR` formatting.

## Impact

P1: Partially mitigated by downstream check, but defense-in-depth suggests validating at the caller site as well for consistency with the RX path fix.

## Fix

Add a bounds check after the call returns (for consistency with RX path):

```c
if (frame_len > sizeof(tx_frame_buf)) {
    LOG_ERR("CRITICAL: lichen_link_tx returned len %zu > buffer %zu",
            frame_len, sizeof(tx_frame_buf));
    // Continue to rely on lichen_lora_l2_tx() rejection, but log it
}
```
